title: Cache Memory: Performance, Construction Types, Write Policies
subtopics: Cache performance parameters (hit ratio, miss ratio, access time)
Extending the cache concept (disk caches, browser caches)

definitions.description: The fraction of memory accesses satisfied from the cache (0 ≤ H ≤ 1).
The fraction of memory accesses not satisfied from the cache; M = 1 - H.
A fixed-size block of consecutive main memory locations brought into the cache together to exploit spatial locality.
Each main-memory line maps to exactly one cache line (determined by the address). Simple but inflexible.
A line can be placed in any cache location. Maximally flexible replacement but slow (parallel comparison required).
Combines direct-mapped and fully-associative: each address maps to a set of n lines (n-way set associative).
Writes go to the cache only; the main memory is updated when the cache line is evicted.
Every write updates both the cache and the main memory simultaneously.
The rule that decides which cache line to evict when the cache is full (e.g., LRU – evict the Least Recently Used line).

formulas.name: Average memory access time
formulas.expression: T = T_c * H + T_m * M  (where T_m is access time on a miss)

formulas.name: Cache performance example
formulas.description: T_c=2ns, T_p=10ns, T_m=11ns, H=95% → T = 2.45ns ≈ 0.245 * T_p  (>4× speedup)

algorithms.name: Direct mapped cache lookup
algorithms.description: Split the address into tag, line selector, and byte offset. Check the cache line at the computed index; compare tags and valid bit.

algorithms.name: Set associative cache lookup
algorithms.description: Map address to a set; check all n ways in parallel for a tag match.

examples.name: Direct mapped cache address decomposition
examples.description: 32-bit address, 211 cache lines × 25 bytes: line selector=11 bits, byte offset=5 bits, tag=16 bits. Example: address 45097373 → tag=688, line=268, byte=29.

examples.name: Direct mapped cache conflict
examples.description: for(i=0;i<1000;i++) a=a+i; where i and a both map to cache line 161 → frequent evictions → many misses.

learning_objectives: Calculate average memory access time using hit ratio
Explain the role of cache lines in exploiting spatial locality
Compare direct mapped, fully associative, and set associative caches
Describe replacement policies and their trade-offs
Identify the cache concept in disk caches and browser caches
